questions.question: What does the CSS clear property do?
options: Specifies whether an element can be next to floated elements
questions.explanation: The clear property determines if an element can be positioned next to floated elements or must be moved below them.

questions.question: What is the default value of the clear property?
questions.explanation: The default value of clear is 'none', meaning the element can be positioned next to floated elements.

questions.question: Which clear value moves an element below both left and right floats?
questions.explanation: clear: both moves the element below all preceding left and right floated elements.

questions.question: What is the difference between clear and clearfix?
options: Clear is for individual elements; clearfix is for parent containers
Clearfix is for inline elements; clear is for blocks
Clear works vertically; clearfix works horizontally
questions.explanation: Clear is applied to individual elements to move them below floats. Clearfix is a technique applied to parent containers to properly contain floated children.

questions.question: What does clear: left do?
options: Moves element below all left-floated elements
Clears the left side of the element
questions.explanation: clear: left pushes the element below any preceding left-floated elements.

questions.question: Can the clear property work on inline elements?
options: Only if they have display: block
questions.explanation: The clear property only affects block-level elements. It does not work on inline elements.

questions.question: What CSS pseudo-element is commonly used in the clearfix technique?
questions.explanation: The standard clearfix uses the ::after pseudo-element with content: '', display: table (or block), and clear: both.

questions.question: How does clear affect an element's margin?
options: It increases the top margin to push the element below floats
questions.explanation: Clear adjusts the element's top margin to push it below preceding floated elements. The margin is calculated based on the height of the tallest preceding float.

questions.question: What happens when an element has clear: both but there are no preceding floats?
options: The element moves to the top
The element stays in normal position
questions.explanation: If there are no preceding floated elements, clear: both has no effect and the element stays in its normal position.

questions.question: What is a common use of clear in multi-row float layouts?
options: Creating row breaks after a certain number of items
questions.explanation: In float-based grids, clear: both is often used to create row breaks, ensuring that subsequent items start on a new line.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used clear value?
questions.explanation: clear: both is the most commonly used value because it clears all floats regardless of direction.

questions.question: What happens when you use clear: right but only left floats exist?
options: The element stays next to left floats
The element moves below left floats too
questions.explanation: clear: right only clears right floats. If only left floats exist, the element can still sit next to them.

questions.question: What display value does the clearfix pseudo-element typically use?
questions.explanation: display: table is commonly used in clearfix because it creates a block formatting context without collapsing margins.

questions.question: Can you use clear with Flexbox or Grid layouts?
options: No, float is ignored in flex/grid contexts so clear has no effect
questions.explanation: Since float is ignored in Flexbox and Grid contexts, clear also has no effect in these layouts.

questions.question: What is an alternative to using clear: both for containing floats?
questions.explanation: overflow: hidden (or auto) on the parent container also creates a block formatting context that contains floats, as an alternative to clearfix.

questions.question: When should you use an empty div with clear: both?
options: For creating row breaks in float layouts
questions.explanation: An empty div with clear: both can be used to force a new row in float-based layouts, though modern approaches prefer clearfix or Grid/Flexbox.

questions.question: What is the initial value of the clear property?
questions.explanation: The initial (default) value of clear is 'none', meaning no clearing is applied.

questions.question: Does clear affect floats that come after the cleared element?
questions.explanation: Clear only affects the positioning relative to preceding (earlier in DOM) floated elements. It doesn't affect floats that come later.

questions.question: What is a modern best practice for clear usage?
options: Use clear: both on every element
Use clear only for text-wrapping scenarios, use Flexbox/Grid for layout
questions.explanation: Modern best practice is to use Flexbox or Grid for layout and reserve clear for managing text wrapping around floated elements.

questions.question: What is the relationship between clear and block formatting context?
options: Clear creates a block formatting context
A block formatting context contains floats, similar to clearfix
questions.explanation: Creating a block formatting context (via overflow: hidden, display: flow-root, etc.) contains floats similarly to clearfix, but by different means.
